description.en: The aim of the experiment is to perform a load test on a single-phase transformer and determine its efficiency and voltage regulation.
The Instructions provide step-by-step guidance on how to perform the simulation.
The AI guide will assist you in performing the simulation accurately at each step.
The CHECK button verifies all the connections to ensure that they are correct.
The AUTO CONNECT button makes all the connections automatically.
The ADD button adds the readings to the observation table.
The PLOT button helps visualise the readings through a graph.
The RESET button refresh the simulation to its initial state for a fresh start.
The PRINT button allows you to print the simulation.

The MCB acts as the main power switch for the experiment. It also protects the circuit by automatically disconnecting the power supply in case of an overload or short circuit. When turned ON, it supplies electrical power to the circuit.

**Rating:** 16A, DP, 240 V AC
**Input Supply:** 230 V AC, 50 Hz

The autotransformer is used to obtain a variable AC output voltage from the 230 V input supply. During the experiment, the input voltage is increased gradually until the required voltage is reached and maintained throughout the load test.

**Ratings:**
**Max. kVA:** 4.05,    **Max. Load:** 15 A 
**Input Voltage:** 240 V, 50Hz
**Output Voltage:** 0 - 240 V AC

The single-phase transformer is the main equipment under test. It is tested under different loading conditions to determine its efficiency and voltage regulation.

**Ratings:**
**Primary Voltage:** 230 V 
**Secondary Voltage:** 115 V
**kVA:**  1

This voltmeter is used to measure the output voltage received from the autotransformer, which is adjusted to 230 V for the experiment. 

**Rating:** 0 - 240 V

This ammeter measures the current flowing through the primary winding of the transformer. During the first reading, it displays the no-load current drawn by the transformer. From the second reading onward, as the load is gradually applied, it displays the primary current corresponding to each load condition.

**Rating:**  0 - 10 A

An AC wattmeter measures the input power drawn by the transformer. During the first reading, it displays the no-load power drawn by the transformer, which mainly represents the core losses. From the second reading onward, as the load is gradually applied, it displays the input power drawn by the transformer under different load conditions. These readings are used to calculate the transformer's efficiency.

**Rating:** 0 - 600 W, 5A, M.F: 2

A lamp load is connected to the secondary side of the transformer to provide a variable resistive load. The load can be increased or decreased by operating the switches, which connect or disconnect a set of lamps. This allows the transformer to be tested under different load conditions.

**Ratings:**  200 W per lamp

This voltmeter measures the voltage across the secondary winding of the transformer. During the first reading, it displays the no-load output voltage. From the second reading onward, as the load is gradually applied, it displays the output voltage corresponding to each load condition. These readings are used to determine the voltage regulation of the transformer.

**Rating:** 0 - 240 V

This ammeter measures the current flowing through the secondary winding of the transformer. Since it is connected in series with the lamp load, it also indicates the load current. During the first reading, it reads zero under no-load conditions. From the second reading onward, it displays the load current corresponding to each load condition. 

**Rating:** 0 - 10 A

The readings obtained from the voltmeters, ammeters, and wattmeter will be recorded in the observation table. These measurements will then be used to calculate the efficiency and voltage regulation of the single-phase transformer.

The Graphs section displays the variation of transformer efficiency and voltage regulation with output power. These graphs will be generated from the recorded readings and help analyze the transformer's performance under different loading conditions.
This is the Formulas section, where the formulas for calculating transformer efficiency and voltage regulation from the observed readings are provided for reference.

The Report button generates a summarised report of the simulation. After completing the observation table and plotting the graphs, generate the report. The report will include the time taken to complete the simulation, simulation summary, the apparatus used, recorded readings, graphs and conclusion.
